# Wei Cheng (Wayne) Chiu

> Machine Learning Engineer focused on LLM inference and GPU performance — hand-written
> CUDA / Tensor-Core kernels, TensorRT-LLM and vLLM serving, and reproducible benchmarks
> on Hopper and Blackwell — alongside multi-agent and RAG systems taken into production.
> Currently a Field Application Engineer at Taiwan AILabs (on-premises LLM deployment),
> previously a Machine Learning Engineer at SYNCROBOTIC (through June 2026), and an
> M.S. Computer Science graduate of NTUST, researching AI/LLM security and
> privacy-preserving ML in Prof. Shao-Jui Wang's lab.

Specialties: applied LLM systems on DGX-class hardware; the NVIDIA inference and
multi-GPU stack (TensorRT-LLM, Triton, NIM, NCCL, CUDA Tensor Cores); and
privacy-preserving ML (federated learning, differential privacy, secure multi-party
computation). The technical notes below report measurements and trade-offs directly;
each one links the public benchmark/code repository behind it.

## Profile

- [About](https://wayne.is-a.dev/): role, focus areas, and contact.
- [CV (PDF)](https://wayne.is-a.dev/cv.pdf): full curriculum vitae — summary, skills, experience, open source, research, education.
- [Experience](https://wayne.is-a.dev/experiences/): industry roles, responsibilities, and education.

## Technical notes (blog)

- [Where tensor-parallel inference hits the NVLink wall](https://wayne.is-a.dev/blog/nccl-nvlink-bandwidth/): bilingual English/Traditional Chinese measurements of 366 GB/s busbw and a roughly 23 μs small-message floor on 4× H100.
- [TensorRT-LLM vs vLLM on 4×H100: The Concurrency Crossover](https://wayne.is-a.dev/blog/notes-trtllm-triton-serving/): bilingual matched-work benchmark where TensorRT-LLM + CUDA Graphs lead at low/mid concurrency and vLLM leads at high concurrency.
- [The 0% RAG result failed the harder test](https://wayne.is-a.dev/blog/rag-groundedness-guardrail/): bilingual N=200 adversarial rerun where the guarded generator hallucinated on 48/100 unanswerable questions; grounded verification catches errors generative judges miss.
- [FedAvg Under Non-IID Data: What DP-SGD and Secure Aggregation Protect](https://wayne.is-a.dev/blog/notes-federated-learning-dp/): bilingual implementations of FedAvg, FedProx, SCAFFOLD, DP-SGD, and secure aggregation, including measured Non-IID limits.
- [From Naive GEMM to WMMA: Where Each CUDA Kernel Stalls](https://wayne.is-a.dev/blog/notes-cuda-tensor-core-gemm/): bilingual English/Traditional Chinese notes on shared-memory tiling, WMMA, and matched-shape comparisons with cuBLAS.

## Public repositories

- [nccl-collectives-bench](https://github.com/waynehacking8/nccl-collectives-bench): NCCL collective benchmarks (all-reduce / all-gather / reduce-scatter) on 4× H100 NVLink, with an analytical roofline that back-validates the measurements.
- [trtllm-triton-serving](https://github.com/waynehacking8/trtllm-triton-serving): production-style LLM serving on TensorRT-LLM tensor-parallel across 4× H100, benchmarked head-to-head against vLLM with controlled methodology, plus a Triton deployment template.
- [nim-agent-blueprint](https://github.com/waynehacking8/nim-agent-blueprint): agentic RAG reference architecture on NVIDIA NIM microservices with a plan→retrieve→validate loop and an eval + OpenTelemetry observability harness.
- [federated-learning-lab](https://github.com/waynehacking8/federated-learning-lab): from-scratch federated learning with DP and secure aggregation, 33/33 tests, literature-cross-validated, including measured Non-IID limits.
